CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-24-2021 Continued To Oct-20-21 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Punitive Damages Allegations; Declaration Of David W. Jennings
Off Calendar Jul-26-2021 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. GABRIELLA CARNEVALE, Esq. of Kern, Segal & Murray (Tel: 415-474-1900) for plaintiff Issraa Juda;
Atty. DAVID W. JENNINGS, Esq. of Demler Armstrong & Rowland LLP (Tel: 562-597-0029) for defendants Alliance United Insurance Company and Kemper Independence Insurance Company.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT ALLIANCE UNITED INSURANCE COMPANY's Motion To Strike Punitive Damages Allegations.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant Alliance United Insurance Company's motion to strike punitive damages allegations is denied. Plaintiff insured alleges that for the sole purpose of avoiding payment for property damages clearly caused by the negligence of an unidentified motorist, defendant unreasonably and in bad faith denied coverage under her automobile insurance policy for property damage to her automobile, while accusing plaintiff of lying about the cause of the damage to her vehicle, and without providing any report or evidence to support its position. (Compl. pars. 10, 15, 20.) Plaintiff alleges further that defendant failed to conduct a full, fair, and thorough investigation of her claim. (Id. par. 31.) Such allegations are sufficient to state a claim for bad faith denial of coverage against an insurer, for which punitive damages may be awarded. (See, e..g. Jordan v. Allstate Ins. Co. (2007) 148 Cal.App.4th 1062, 1080 [insured may be entitled to recover punitive damages if she can prove that insurer denied or delayed payment of policy benefits unreasonable or without proper cause and, in doing so, was guilty of malice, oppression or fraud].) Contrary to defendant's contention, these allegations go well beyond mere negligence or defendant simply "asserting its side of a coverage dispute." Whether plaintiff can prove the allegations to the statutory standard of clear and convincing evidence presents an issue for summary adjudication or trial that cannot be decided on a motion to strike.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Rose Miller, CSR No. 5041, Email: RMillerCSR@gmail.com. (302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Punitive Damages Allegations; Declaration Of David W. Jennings
